verdict_reasoning: EREG is synthesized as a type I transmembrane precursor (169 aa, including 21 aa TM domain) that traffics to the cell surface, where it can engage in juxtacrine signaling. However, the TM precursor stage is transient and unstable; ADAM17 constitutively cleaves pro-EREG to release the soluble 46 aa mature form, which is the dominant biological actor. The membrane-anchored stage is real and functional but short-lived; the protein reaches the outer leaflet during the brief TM-precursor phase before rapid proteolytic release into soluble form. This dual-localization topology (stable TM precursor with transient surface residence, soluble form as canonical actor) maps to contextual surface accessibility.
